behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local lint logic over template text, not a module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA: none required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not hide this because no external dependency is needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing real harness wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative prompt-template strings (clean and leaking variants copied from SKILL.md phase instructions) into the lint function and assert it rejects leakage patterns and accepts safe templates.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly). REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess/OS primitive required beyond local wiring. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only template logic can miss leakage introduced during assembly. Integration-brake: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction path in the harness with production-like fixtures/interpolation and assert the final assembled prompt text does not contain phase-specific SKILL.md instruction leakage tokens.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary includes both file-loaded context handoff and model behavior conditioned on that context. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model behavior being driven by loaded SKILL.md context; deterministic stubs can mask this. MULTI-STEP: single model decision path, not a multi-agent pipeline. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules," which requires real model-dependent behavior, not just wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation that loads real plugin/skill files, send a user-style prompt, and assert observed model behavior aligns with skill-derived constraints while prompt text itself does not hard-code those rules.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is harness orchestration/state handoff between prompt input and skill-file variant selection. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP: this behavior checks harness ability to swap context with unchanged prompt text; model quality is not required to verify capability. Integration-brake: (a) no, (b) no for this behavior, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness twice with identical user prompt text while switching skill-file source (normal vs degraded/missing variant) via fixture/config, and assert prompt text remains identical while selected skill context source changes as configured.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on real model behavior changing when skill guidance is removed/degraded. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model output could still pass and hide the regression. MULTI-STEP: single prompt/response behavioral check, not a multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior requires that degradation causes behavioral failure under same prompt, which depends on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test with baseline SKILL.md and degraded SKILL.md using identical user prompt; assert baseline passes structural behavior checks and degraded variant fails them.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug occurs across modules (model call wrapper, artifact writer, parser). REAL-INFRA: local filesystem artifact is sufficient; no real network/subprocess requirement for this check itself. LLM-DEP: content quality not required; can use deterministic stubbed output to verify pre-parse capture ordering. Integration-brake: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument harness run to emit known raw response, then assert diagnostic artifact contains the unparsed raw text and that artifact write occurs before parser/extractor invocation (e.g., via timestamps/hooks/order assertions).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: primary failure is assertion logic design (structure-aware vs keyword-only) within local validation code. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required to validate checker strictness. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on assertion semantics, not cross-module execution.
behaviors.test_description: Provide crafted response fixtures: one with required keywords but wrong schema path/role placement, and one structurally correct; assert validator fails the former and passes the latter.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: explicitly "under live model execution" and depends on real output variability/regression shape. MOCK-HIDE: fixed fixtures can miss real malformed-yet-keyword-present outputs. MULTI-STEP: single live generation plus assertion, not multi-agent pipeline. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior does not describe multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model smoke test and evaluate outputs with both keyword-only and structural validators; assert at least one live regression case passes keyword check but fails structural constraints.
